authoritative_sources.title: ITAR Part 120 and Part 126
authoritative_sources.authority_rationale: Primary statutory-classification framework governing defense articles, technical data, defense services, and controls by destination and parties.
authoritative_sources.workflow_stage: Jurisdiction intake, export classification, and embargo/recipient controls

authoritative_sources.title: DFARS 252.225-7048 Export-Controlled Items
authoritative_sources.source_class: Recognized workflow references tied directly to the lane
authoritative_sources.authority_rationale: Contract-level rule source for defense work where export control clauses, obligations, and handoff requirements are enforceable in procurement context.
authoritative_sources.workflow_stage: Contract-bound control gating and hold-release evidence assembly

authoritative_sources.title: DFARS 225.7901-4 Contract Clause
authoritative_sources.source_class: Recognized workflow references tied directly to the lane
authoritative_sources.authority_rationale: Defines where export-control clauses belong in solicitations and therefore determines record dependencies and authority boundaries in this lane.

authoritative_sources.title: BIS Export Administration Regulations
authoritative_sources.source_class: Evidence or control standards relevant to the lane
authoritative_sources.authority_rationale: Primary dual-use export framework for item and software classification, license exceptions, and licensing need decisions.

authoritative_sources.title: BIS Recordkeeping and Compliance Requirements
authoritative_sources.source_class: Evidence or control standards relevant to the lane
authoritative_sources.authority_rationale: Controls what records must be retained and what level of documentary sufficiency is expected for compliance and audit review.
authoritative_sources.workflow_stage: Evidence sufficiency checks and nonconformance review

authoritative_sources.title: BIS EAR Licensing Guidance
authoritative_sources.source_class: Industry terminology and record normalization source
authoritative_sources.authority_rationale: Clarifies practical licensing workflow, jurisdictional inputs, and step order used to support robust pre-clearance decisions.

authoritative_sources.title: OFAC Sanctions List Service
authoritative_sources.source_class: Evidence or control standards relevant to the lane
authoritative_sources.authority_rationale: Primary reference for sanctions and denied-party screening outcomes that materially alter transfer legality and routing.

authoritative_sources.title: NAICS 3364 Sector Framework
authoritative_sources.source_class: Industry terminology and record normalization source
authoritative_sources.authority_rationale: Defines defense-industry vocabulary and business categorization used for tenant and domain artifact normalization.

workflow_stages.name: Control-intake and evidence capture
workflow_stages.description: Capture mission context, item/technology definition, destination, end-user, and ownership chain before any clearance claim.
source_classes: Evidence or control standards relevant to the lane

workflow_stages.name: Jurisdiction and license pathway assembly
workflow_stages.description: Apply ITAR/EAR classification logic, denied-party screening, and sanctions constraints to determine whether a case is blockable, licensable, or release-restricted.
source_classes: Industry terminology and record normalization source

workflow_stages.name: Contractual and records-boundary enforcement
workflow_stages.description: Map required DFARS contract obligations, open holds, findings, CAPA history, and release requirements to a hold/release recommendation.
source_classes: Recognized workflow references tied directly to the lane
Evidence or control standards relevant to the lane

workflow_stages.name: Dependency, conflict, and escalation handling
workflow_stages.description: Detect unavailable systems, policy conflicts, repeat findings, and adjacent-authority shifts and route handoffs without autonomous override.
source_classes: Recognized workflow references tied directly to the lane
Evidence or control standards relevant to the lane

workflow_stages.name: Tenant-adapted closeout and traceability
workflow_stages.description: Produce explicit assumption records and tenant-aligned evidence outputs before final status, especially when mission definitions change outcomes.
source_classes: Industry terminology and record normalization source
Evidence or control standards relevant to the lane

artifacts: Export-control request packet and classification worksheet
License applications and license status logs
Denied-party screening outputs and sanctions review notes
Hold/Release decision and exception signoff records
systems_of_record: Export classification and screening tool output repositories
Tenant mission dictionary and policy configuration stores

allowed: Issue evidence-backed in-scope/out-of-scope and export-control posture recommendations.
Map required artifacts, holds, and nonconformance dependencies.
Escalate to adjacent specialists when legal, licensing final authority, sanctions counsel, safety, or procurement override decisions are required.
escalate: Missing tenant authority records materially affect outcome.
Sanctions, legal interpretation, or safety-critical escalation is required.
Required records are blocked by missing systems-of-record dependencies.
Public and tenant policies conflict on release classification.
refuse: Requests to bypass screening, license checks, or hold/release controls.
Requests for final legal or procurement-award authority.
Requests to close repeated incidents without corrected corrective action.

domain_failure_modes: Over-generalized generic compliance behavior that ignores USML/ECCN intersection complexity.
Inadequate denied-party or sanctions review before clearance recommendation.
Release decisions based on incomplete hold registry or correction-history evidence.
Uninformed autonomy in legal/safety or engineering-safety adjudication.
Ignoring tenant mission dictionaries that change risk thresholds and outcomes.

scenario_family_coverage.scenario_family: Boundary case that would overgeneralize from the industry name
Request that requires a more specialized adjacent lane
Safety or quality evidence is incomplete or contradictory
Release is requested while a hold or unresolved issue exists
Incident handling requires licensed, safety, or formal QA authority
A required control is missing evidence
Tenant-specific adaptation handling that preserves public baseline

unresolved_ambiguities.impact: Different program sites may classify mission-essential status and transfer urgency differently.
unresolved_ambiguities.handling: Treat as tenant-required context with mandatory confirmation before final recommendation.

unresolved_ambiguities.topic: Preferred handling for repeated sanction-sensitive findings
unresolved_ambiguities.impact: Some programs stop work, others route to corrective-action loop.
unresolved_ambiguities.handling: Require tenant escalation owner guidance and hold posture in all ambiguous recurrence cases.

unresolved_ambiguities.topic: Source refresh trigger for sanctions feeds
unresolved_ambiguities.impact: Frequency of updates differs by tenant integration method and source APIs.
unresolved_ambiguities.handling: Use service-level recency checks before each disposition and request re-run on stale responses.

notes: Public baseline includes ITAR, EAR, DFARS, and sanctions references and is scoped through defense-industry workflows.
All required source classes are used for workflow, evidence, terminology, and failure-handling coverage.
No blocking unresolved ambiguity remains after converting open points to explicit tenant context requirements and escalation conditions.
